2604.13538.title: Synthesizing Instruction-Tuning Datasets with Contrastive Decoding
2604.13538.paper_summary: Using responses generated by high-performing large language models (LLMs) for instruction tuning has become a widely adopted approach. However, the existing literature overlooks a property of LLM-generated responses: they conflate world knowledge acquired during pre-training with instruction-following capabilities acquired during post-training. We hypothesize that disentangling the instruction-following capabilities from pre-trained knowledge improves the effectiveness of instruction tuning. To this end, we propose CoDIT, a method that applies contrastive decoding between a post-trained model and its pre-trained counterpart during response generation. The method suppresses pre-trained knowledge shared between the two models while amplifying the instruction-following behavior acquired via post-training, resulting in responses that more purely reflect instruction-following capabilities. Experiment results demonstrate that models trained on datasets constructed via CoDIT consistently outperform those trained on directly generated responses. Training on our datasets also yields better performance than on existing publicly available instruction-tuning datasets across multiple benchmarks. Furthermore, we theoretically and empirically show that CoDIT can be interpreted as distilling the chat vector from parameter space to text space, enabling the transfer of instruction-tuning capabilities across models of different architectures.
2604.13538.paper_summary_zh: 使用高效能大型語言模型（LLMs）生成的回應進行指令調整已成為一種廣泛採用的方法。<lb/>然而，現有文獻忽略了LLM生成回應的一個特性：它們將在預訓練期間獲得的世界知識與在後訓練期間獲得的遵循指令能力混為一談。<lb/>我們假設，將遵循指令的能力與預訓練知識分開，可以提高指令調整的有效性。<lb/>為此，我們提出了CoDIT，一種在回應生成過程中對後訓練模型和其預訓練對應模型進行對比解碼的方法。<lb/>該方法抑制了兩個模型之間共享的預訓練知識，同時增強了通過後訓練獲得的遵循指令行為，從而使回應更純粹地反映遵循指令的能力。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，基於CoDIT構建的數據集訓練的模型在性能上始終優於基於直接生成回應訓練的模型。<lb/>在我們的數據集上進行訓練的模型在多個基準測試中也比現有的公共可用指令調整數據集表現更佳。<lb/>此外，我們從理論和實證上表明，CoDIT可以被解釋為將聊天向量從參數空間提煉到文本空間，從而實現不同架構模型之間的指令調整能力轉移。
2604.13538.author: Tatsuya Ichinose et.al.
2604.13538.authors: Tatsuya Ichinose, Youmi Ma, Masanari Oi, Ryuto Koike, Naoaki Okazaki
